questions.prompt: Which compiler stage should reject a use of an undeclared identifier after syntax has parsed successfully?
questions.explanation: The parser can recognize the identifier-shaped expression, but scoped name resolution determines whether it denotes a declaration. Keeping stages distinct improves diagnostics.

questions.prompt: For a simple PC-relative relocation, which expression is commonly represented by S + A - P?

choices.A: Symbol address plus addend minus relocation-place address
choices.B: Section size plus alignment minus file size
choices.C: Stack pointer plus argument count minus page size

questions.explanation: S is the resolved symbol, A the encoded/additional addend, and P the address of the relocation place. The result still requires width and range checking.

questions.prompt: Why can a forward-only byte loop implement memcpy but not general memmove?

choices.B: Overlapping destination at a higher address can overwrite source bytes before they are read

questions.explanation: When ranges overlap and destination starts inside the source at a higher address, copying backward preserves unread source bytes. Direction depends on the range relationship.

questions.prompt: In a transmit descriptor ring, when may software safely reuse a descriptor buffer?

choices.B: Only after the controller has explicitly returned ownership or completion
choices.C: Whenever producer and consumer indexes differ

questions.explanation: Ownership, not elapsed time, establishes whether the controller may still read the buffer. Reuse before completion creates data races and frame corruption.

questions.prompt: Which property is not provided by an ordinary non-cryptographic boot image checksum?

choices.A: Detection of many accidental bit errors
choices.B: Evidence that all expected bytes arrived when length is also checked
choices.C: Authentication that the image came from an authorized producer

questions.explanation: An attacker can replace both image and ordinary checksum. Authentication requires a keyed MAC or signature plus key and rollback policy.
